citations.narrative:
The Continency Plan has not been reviewed by the licensee on at least an annual basis. The last review of the
Contingency Plan was completed in December 2024; the Plan was not reviewed in 2025.
Per this Section, all exhibitors must review their Continency Plan on at least an annual basis to ensure that it adequately
addresses potential emergency situations that could be detrimental to the health and well-being of the animals they must
maintain documentation of their annual reviews. The Licensee needs to review the Contingency Plan and document the
review. Correct by 3/6/26.

The cat enclosures have a fabric scratching pad square mounted to one wall of the enclosures. In one enclosure, the top
portion of the scratching pad had detached from the wall and approximately 2-3 inches of the top edge of the fabric pad
was hanging down. In another enclosure, a portion of the scratching pad approximately 2x2 inches in size was torn from
the top right corner and was hanging from the pad.
Per this Section, all surfaces must be maintained on a regular basis and fixtures/objects within the facility that cannot be
readily cleaned or sanitized must be replaced when worn. The fabric scratching pad squares in the two cat enclosures are
worn and need to be replaced to maintain the wall surface of the two cat enclosures. Correct by 3/6/26.

The walls of the goat enclosure are made of a type of particle board sheets that have an outermost, white-colored layer
that has a smooth surface. There was damage noted on the left side of the back wall at floor level; an irregularly shaped
portion of the outer smooth layer had come off, and the exposed inner particle board layer was cracked.
Per this Section, housing facilities shall be maintained in good repair. The Licensee needs to address the damaged wall in
the goat pen to maintain the enclosure in good repair. Correct by 3/6/26.
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representative.
*END OF REPORT*
n

narrative:
2.134(b) 
   
Contingency planning. 
The Continency Plan has not been reviewed by the licensee on at least an annual basis. The last review of the 
Contingency Plan was completed in December 2024; the Plan was not reviewed in 2025.  
 
Per this Section, all exhibitors must review their Continency Plan on at least an annual basis to ensure that it adequately 
addresses potential emergency situations that could be detrimental to the health and well-being of the animals they must 
maintain documentation of their annual reviews. The Licensee needs to review the Contingency Plan and document the 
review. Correct by 3/6/26. 
3.1(c)(2) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
The cat enclosures have a fabric scratching pad square mounted to one wall of the enclosures. In one enclosure, the top 
portion of the scratching pad had detached from the wall and approximately 2-3 inches of the top edge of the fabric pad 
was hanging down. In another enclosure, a portion of the scratching pad approximately 2x2 inches in size was torn from 
the top right corner and was hanging from the pad.  
 
Per this Section, all surfaces must be maintained on a regular basis and fixtures/objects within the facility that cannot be 
readily cleaned or sanitized must be replaced when worn. The fabric scratching pad squares in the two cat enclosures are 
worn and need to be replaced to maintain the wall surface of the two cat enclosures. Correct by 3/6/26. 
3.125(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The walls of the goat enclosure are made of a type of particle board sheets that have an outermost, white-colored layer 
that has a smooth surface. There was damage noted on the left side of the back wall at floor level; an irregularly shaped 
portion of the outer smooth layer had come off, and the exposed inner particle board layer was cracked.  
 
Per this Section, housing facilities shall be maintained in good repair. The Licensee needs to address the damaged wall in 
the goat pen to maintain the enclosure in good repair. Correct by 3/6/26.

This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representative.  
 
*END OF REPORT*
